techniques.evidence_quote: This particular incident involved the use of compromised IAM user access keys (AKIA) to gain initial access to a cloud account and invoke Bedrock models.
we discovered an IAM user operating from a Proton VPN IP address
the attacker used the high-level permissions of the IAM user to create new IAM users, along with IAM access keys and a policy granting Bedrock permissions
the attacker used the high-level permissions of the IAM user to create new IAM users, along with IAM access keys and a policy granting Bedrock permissions
the attacker used the high-level permissions of the IAM user to create new IAM users, along with IAM access keys and a policy granting Bedrock permissions
The attacker created two additional IAM users following the same naming scheme and repeated the process with them.
along with IAM access keys and a policy granting Bedrock permissions
made numerous unsuccessful attempts to invoke Bedrock models, likely using a Python script
attempts to invoke Bedrock models... the attacker tried to invoke the model again, this time using the new user credentials

attack_chain.description: JINX-2401 used stolen/compromised IAM user long-term access keys (AKIA type) to authenticate to a victim AWS account, gaining initial access.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: This particular incident involved the use of compromised IAM user access keys (AKIA) to gain initial access to a cloud account and invoke Bedrock models.

attack_chain.description: The attacker routed all activity through Proton VPN exit nodes to obscure their true origin IP address.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: we discovered an IAM user operating from a Proton VPN IP address

attack_chain.description: Using the compromised IAM credentials, the attacker made repeated attempts to invoke Amazon Bedrock LLM models (e.g., Anthropic Claude variants) via Python scripts.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: made numerous unsuccessful attempts to invoke Bedrock models, likely using a Python script

attack_chain.description: After Bedrock invocation failures, the attacker leveraged the Administrator-level compromised IAM user to create new IAM users following a predictable naming pattern (regex: ^[A-Z][a-z]{5}[0-9]{3}$) with associated access keys.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker used the high-level permissions of the IAM user to create new IAM users, along with IAM access keys and a policy granting Bedrock permissions. The newly created IAM users and policy names adhered to the same naming patterns we had previously identified with this attacker.

attack_chain.description: The attacker created IAM access keys for the newly created IAM users and attached a custom policy ('New_Policy') granting bedrock:InvokeModel, bedrock:InvokeModelWithResponseStream, and bedrock:GetModelInvocationLoggingConfiguration permissions.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: along with IAM access keys and a policy granting Bedrock permissions

attack_chain.description: The attacker created a console login profile for the new IAM user to complete the LLM model use-case agreement process via the AWS Console, using the PutUseCaseForModelAccess and CreateFoundationModelAgreement API calls.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker created a console profile for the new IAM user, likely to complete the LLM agreement process. This activity was evidenced by the AWS API call PutUseCaseForModelAccess, performed via the console. Subsequently, the user attempted to request access to specific models using the CreateFoundationModelAgreement API call.

attack_chain.description: The attacker attempted to invoke Bedrock LLM models again using the newly created IAM user credentials. These attempts failed due to Service Control Policies (SCPs) blocking CreateFoundationModelAgreement and InvokeModel API calls. The attacker repeated this cycle with two additional newly created IAM users.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker tried to invoke the model again, this time using the new user credentials. These attempts failed as well, due to the SCP blocking the CreateFoundationModelAgreement and InvokeModel API calls. Undeterred, the attacker created two additional IAM users following the same naming scheme and repeated the process with them.

initial_access_vector: Stolen/compromised IAM user long-term access keys (AKIA) used to authenticate to victim AWS accounts
persistence_mechanism: Creation of multiple new IAM users with dedicated access keys and a permissive Bedrock policy ('New_Policy'), plus console login profiles, to maintain operational access independent of the initially compromised credential
privilege_escalation: The compromised IAM user already held Administrator Access permissions; attacker leveraged these to create new IAM users and attach custom policies granting Bedrock invocation rights
